ocr.text: In the context of advancing sustainable waste management practices, Veolia Environnement continues to enhance its carbon
offset project pipeline while effectively managing hazardous waste disposal volumes. The data presented in the accompanying
table reflects performance metrics for Q2 2023 and Q3 2023, highlighting critical areas such as CAPEX and OPEX. Notably,
the CAPEX for Q3 2023 was £50M, demonstrating our ongoing investment in innovative solutions.
FinancialOverview  Revenue(GBP)  ExpenditureSummary  NetIncome(GBP)  ProjectMetrics
Q22023  Q32023  Change(%)  CAPEX(GBP)  OPEX(GBP)  ProfitMargin(%)  CarbonCredits  RevenuesfromCredits  TotalProjects
In parallel, the OPEX stood at £35M, indicating our commitment to operational efficiency.

The projected change in revenues from credits for the same period shows a steady increase of 5.00%, with revenues from
credits reaching £7M. Our profit margin improved to 14.29% for the £150M revenue target in Q3 2023, which was reported at
£175M, underscoring the progress made in our sustainability initiatives. Additionally, the hazardous waste management
segment recorded a collection volume of 5000 tons, with an impressive recycling recovery rate of 75%. Furthermore, the active
landfill operations, totaling 750 tons, underscore the dual approach of reducing waste through recycling while managing
residuals responsibly. These metrics collectively illustrate Veolia’s robust strategy in environmental services and waste
management.
